text: The laparoscopic camera is introduced through the umbilical port, providing a view of the small bowel, terminal ileum, cecum, and ascending colon. The surgeon uses a grasper to hold the appendix.

The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to cut the mesoappendix, providing a sharp incision and good hemostasis. The appendix and mesoappendix are clearly visible, and care is taken to avoid injuring the cecum while progressing towards the base of the appendix.

The surgeon reaches the base of the appendix and uses a knot pusher with chromic catgut to ligate the base at the roder's node. The appendix is held and pushed upwards as the proximal knot is tightened, followed by the application of the distal knot. The color of the appendix changes due to devascularization.

The surgeon cuts between the two ligated knots on the appendix, using suction to remove any spillage of bowel contents. Free fluid is visible in the pouch of Douglas below the uterus, which is being aspirated. The appendix is then removed through the umbilical port.
The surgeon performs a bowel walk to inspect for any abnormalities in Meckel's diverticulum, ensuring thorough examination as part of the appendectomy procedure.
The surgeon presents the retrieved appendix specimen, which has been removed through the umbilical port.
